nature.trend: Week over week, the Thai basil still reads as a slow vegetative fill-in rather than a breakout week: moderate canopy, uneven vigor, mild stretch, and no fresh high-confidence pest or disease signal.
progress: Compared with the prior weekly anchor, the tray still appears fuller overall.
Within this specific week, day-to-day biology changes looked modest rather than dramatic.
Midweek confidence dropped because fresh saved images were temporarily missing before later capture recovery.

plamp.reliability: Reliability was mixed: hourly camera runs stayed active, the evidence pipeline looked broken during the May 10 to May 12 checks, then saved captures resumed in the current grow path by May 10/11.
events: plamp.log shows uninterrupted Picamera2 start/stop cycles through 2026-05-13 00:49Z.
Fresh saved captures are now present again under the data-path grow tree for 2026-05-10 through 2026-05-13, clearing the earlier no-files-landing symptom.
A controller fault hit on 2026-05-11 around 08:24 HST: pump_n_lights cycled through applying, reconnecting, disconnected, and connected on /dev/ttyACM0 twice before recovery.
The older daytime under-bright concern remains unresolved and should be rechecked now that saved captures are back.

completed: Ran repeated daily heartbeat checks and kept grow summaries current.
Monitored plamp.log for camera continuity and controller faults.
Confirmed that saved captures are landing again in the current data-path grow tree.
next_actions: Physically verify whether daytime light output looks correct now that fresh saved captures are back.
Inspect the Pico/controller path for the 2026-05-11 reconnect churn on /dev/ttyACM0.
Use the restored capture stream to reassess whether canopy fill is improving or merely holding steady.

risks: /dev/ttyACM0 instability could still cause missed light or pump cycles even when the controller reconnects.
The crop model is still conservative because the week included an evidence-gap scare and no strong growth burst.
mitigations: Keep checking plamp.log for new reconnect or apply faults and compare them against timer behavior.
Use the restored saved captures to rebuild confidence in daytime light-state and crop progression before changing strategy.
